when the Moon is in that sign. Suppose the conception took
place when Aries was rising and when the Moon was in the
tenth house Makara. lfthe Moon is in the tenth dvadasamsa,
that sign will be Tula. Birth takes place when the Moon is in
the tenth from Tula, namely Cancer.
Birth is said to be in the tenth month after conception.
Since the Moon covers a sign in about 2 1/4 days, one can fix
the date ofbirth within this period.
A sign has thirty degrees or 1800 minutes (liptas). Each
sign has nine quarters ofthe constellations. Dividing 1800 by
2 1/4 we get 800 liptas (Kalas, minutes) for each star. Each
dvadasamsa of a sign covers 150 liptas or kalas. Suppose the
Moon is in the fifth dvadasamsa ofMithuna. That is, the Moon
is between 600 and 750 liptas. It is less than 800 li ptas which
cover one constellation. The Moon is then in the second quar
ter of Ardra, if the Moon is on the 700th lipta of Mithuna, he
has traversed one hundred liptas in the second quarter of
Ardra.
